evals.prompt: Decompose this into subagent tasks: refactor AuthService to use a token bucket rate limiter and update the auth docs. Don't run anything yet.
evals.expected_output: A task manifest JSON with 3 tasks (ratelimit library, auth integration, docs update). Library and docs are named subagents; auth integration is a fork because it's tightly coupled to the parent's AuthService understanding. Library and auth-integration use worktrees; docs uses shared cwd. The manifest validates against templates/task-manifest.schema.json and is written to .claude/squad/runs/<run-id>/manifest.json.

evals.prompt: This is a single-file typo fix. Decompose it into subagent tasks.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses and tells the user this is a single-agent task. No manifest is written.

evals.prompt: Create squad worktrees for run auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads .claude/squad/runs/auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24/manifest.json, creates a git worktree per task with worktree: true on a squad/auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24/<task-id> branch, applies .worktreeinclude if present, symlinks worktree.symlinkDirectories, and writes worktree-map.json. Only touches squad/* branches.

evals.prompt: Clean up the squad worktrees for run auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks every worktree for dirty state, refuses and escalates if any are dirty, otherwise removes each worktree, deletes the squad/* branches, prunes refs/squad-incoming/<run-id>/*, and archives the run dir under .claude/squad/runs/_archived/.

evals.prompt: Spawn the subagents for run auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24 and merge them onto an integration branch.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates squad/auth-rate-limit-2026-04-24/integration from base_ref, dispatches children per the manifest (fork for tightly-coupled tasks, named for self-contained ones) with each one receiving the child-prompt template, collects child-return JSONs, cherry-picks each child's commits onto integration in merge_order, runs the validation gate between children, writes squad-run.json, and ends with 'Integration branch ready: ... Run /commit to ship.' Never pushes.

evals.prompt: Spawn this manifest — two parallel tasks both edit src/auth/service.ts.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects the shared non-read-only file in the same dispatch group, refuses with a file-ownership violation message, and tells the user to re-run /decompose so the two tasks are sequenced via dependencies.

evals.prompt: Spawn the manifest. On conflict, use the auto-resolver.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects --auto-resolve in the arguments. If .claude/agents/squad-resolver.md exists, on cherry-pick conflict it dispatches a named squad-resolver subagent with an objective scoped to the unmerged paths and continues after the resolver returns. If the profile is missing, it falls back to pause-and-ask with a clear warning.

evals.prompt: Orchestrate the full squad flow for: add a trivial helper, add a smoke test, update the README.
evals.expected_output: Runs /decompose, stops at checkpoint 1 with the manifest summary, runs /worktree after the user approves, stops at checkpoint 2, runs /spawn, and ends with the integration-branch-ready message pointing the user at /commit. On abort with worktrees created (and no --keep), auto-invokes /worktree cleanup:<run-id>.

evals.prompt: Orchestrate this goal but only decompose — don't touch worktrees or spawn.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes --dry-run (or treats the phrasing as a dry run), invokes /decompose, prints the manifest summary, and exits cleanly without creating worktrees or dispatching children.

evals.prompt: Set up squad in this repo.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects CLAUDE.md (or creates it), appends the block from templates/squad-claude-block.md between <!-- skrrt:squad --> markers (or replaces existing block content between markers), suggests (does not modify) adding .claude/squad/ to .gitignore, and notes whether the ship plugin is present so the user knows the downstream shipping path.
